key_highlights: Strong May jobs report (172,000 jobs added) fuels Fed rate-hike bets, triggering broad market sell-off led by tech and Nasdaq.
Massive rotation out of tech and AI stocks into value, banks, and retail; Dow surges 800 points while Nasdaq sinks 3%.
Oil prices fall sharply on demand destruction fears and stalled peace talks in the Middle East; Hormuz traffic near zero.
SpaceX IPO draws massive oversubscription at $1.77 trillion valuation, but analysts flag 'borderline dishonest' filing.
AI spending continues unabated, with Morgan Stanley seeing AI-related credit deals expanding to 15% of all deals; energy infrastructure concerns rise.
Consumer spending remains resilient across income bands per Mastercard CEO, but jobless claims hit highest since Iran war began.
Gold slumps most in two months as dollar strengthens on rate-hike expectations.
Credit markets show signs of froth, with titans warning of shakeout on deals that 'don't make sense'.

sectors.trend: Sharp sell-off driven by rate-hike fears and profit-taking after massive run-up; Broadcom and Ciena slammed on earnings misses, while memory-chip stocks like Micron and Sandisk also fell. Nasdaq 100 dropped 3%.
sectors.implications: Near-term headwinds as rising yields compress valuations; but long-term AI spending momentum remains intact. Rotation out of mega-cap tech into value and cyclical sectors.

sectors.trend: Oil prices fall on demand destruction fears and stalled peace talks; Hormuz traffic remains near zero. Gran Tierra Energy seen as cheap cash flow play.
sectors.implications: Short-term bearish on crude due to demand concerns and potential de-escalation; but geopolitical risk remains elevated. Oil plays may offer value if conflict reignites.

sectors.trend: Banks and retail stocks rally as investors rotate out of tech; Dow surges 800 points. Credit markets show signs of froth with warnings of shakeout; private credit under scrutiny.
sectors.implications: Beneficiaries of higher rates and rotation; but credit quality concerns linger. Opportunity in select bank stocks and credit-focused funds.

sectors.trend: Mastercard CEO reports spending growth across all income bands; Cinemark reports highest-ever domestic box office for May. Lululemon tumbles on trimmed guidance and founder drama.
sectors.implications: Consumer remains resilient, but selective weakness in some names. Travel and entertainment stocks may benefit from continued spending.

sectors.trend: Genmab gets buy rating; AstraZeneca broadens US growth push. Ebola pioneer comments on disease risks.
sectors.implications: Steady sector with defensive characteristics; biotech may offer selective opportunities.

sectors.trend: Data center wave bodes well for PG&E; Quanta Services upgraded by Oppenheimer. Energy failures could doom AI euphoria per Telegraph.
sectors.implications: Power and infrastructure plays are critical for AI buildout; long-term demand drivers remain strong.

sectors.trend: DraftKings to benefit from broader growth across prediction markets; casino culture in markets noted by Schwab's Sonders.
sectors.implications: Growth in legalized betting and prediction markets offers tailwinds for DKNG and similar platforms.

key_themes: Rate-Hike Fears Dominate: Strong jobs data fuels expectations of Fed rate hike, causing broad sell-off in bonds and equities, especially tech.
Massive Rotation: Investors moving from growth/tech to value, banks, retail, and energy; Dow outperforms Nasdaq sharply.
AI Spending Continues Unabated: Despite tech sell-off, AI-related funding and credit deals expand; Morgan Stanley sees 15% of all credit deals tied to AI.
Geopolitical Risks Persist: Middle East tensions (Hormuz, US-Iran war) and stalled peace talks keep oil and defense in focus.
Credit Market Froth: Warnings from credit titans about deals that 'don't make sense'; private credit under regulatory scrutiny.
SpaceX IPO Mania: Oversubscribed IPO at $1.77 trillion valuation draws both excitement and skepticism from analysts.
Consumer Resilience: Spending holds up across income bands, but jobless claims rise to highest since Iran war began, signaling potential cracks.
Energy Infrastructure Concerns: Power grid and energy failures could constrain AI growth; nuclear watchdog eyes stronger defenses after UAE attack.

risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce duration in bond portfolios; favor floating-rate debt and short-term treasuries. Increase cash allocation.
Trim overvalued AI and semiconductor positions; rotate into value and defensive sectors. Use stop-losses on high-beta names.
Maintain exposure to energy and defense stocks; hedge with gold or oil futures. Monitor Hormuz traffic and peace talks.
Avoid low-quality credit; favor investment-grade bonds and high-quality dividend stocks. Monitor private credit exposure.
Invest in utility and infrastructure companies benefiting from data center demand (e.g., PCG, PWR). Monitor grid reliability.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: SpaceX IPO Hype and Regulatory Risk
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid chasing IPO at current valuation; wait for post-IPO stabilization. Monitor regulatory developments.
Focus on essential consumer staples and discount retailers; avoid highly discretionary names. Monitor jobless claims and retail sales.

opportunities.recommendation: Rotate into value and cyclical sectors (banks, retail, energy) as rate-hike fears drive rotation out of tech.
opportunities.rationale: Strong jobs data and rising yields favor financials and value stocks. Dow's 800-point surge confirms rotation.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy infrastructure and utility stocks benefiting from AI data center demand.
opportunities.rationale: Ongoing data center wave and AI buildout require massive power and infrastructure investment. PG&E and Quanta Services are well-positioned.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate high-quality dividend stocks with 11%+ yield as defensive income plays.
opportunities.rationale: UBS picks dividend stocks with double-digit upside; in a volatile rate environment, high-yield defensive stocks provide income and stability.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider selective AI infrastructure plays on pullback (e.g., Marvell, Analog Devices).
opportunities.rationale: Raymond James raised Marvell target to $235; JPMorgan raised Analog Devices target. AI spending is secular, and pullbacks offer entry points.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy DraftKings on prediction market growth theme.
opportunities.rationale: Broader growth across prediction markets and legalized betting provides tailwinds for DKNG.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase cash allocation and reduce exposure to high-beta tech and AI stocks.
defensive_moves.rationale: Rate-hike fears and rotation out of tech suggest near-term downside for growth stocks. Cash provides optionality to buy dips.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against geopolitical risk with gold or oil futures.
defensive_moves.rationale: Middle East tensions remain elevated; Hormuz traffic near zero. Gold slumped on strong jobs data but remains a hedge.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce duration in bond portfolios; favor short-term treasuries and floating-rate debt.
defensive_moves.rationale: Rising yields and potential Fed hike make long-duration bonds vulnerable. Floating-rate notes and T-bills offer safety.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid chasing SpaceX IPO at current valuation; wait for post-IPO stabilization.
defensive_moves.rationale: Analysts flag 'borderline dishonest' filing and $1.77 trillion valuation is extreme. Oversubscription may lead to initial pop but risk of correction is high.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Bearish on equities, especially tech, as Fed rate-hike expectations dominate. Expect continued rotation into value, banks, and energy. Bond yields likely to rise further, pressuring growth stocks. Oil may remain volatile due to geopolitical uncertainty. Gold could recover if rate expectations stabilize.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Neutral to slightly positive. AI spending and data center buildout provide long-term growth drivers. Infrastructure and utility stocks benefit. Credit market risks may materialize, but selective opportunities in high-quality dividend stocks and value sectors. Geopolitical risks (Middle East, US-Iran) remain wildcards.
key_catalysts: Fed meeting and rate decision in June/July 2026
Progress in Middle East peace talks and Hormuz traffic resumption
SpaceX IPO pricing and first-day trading performance
Q2 earnings season, especially for tech and AI companies
US inflation data (CPI, PCE) for May and June
Jobless claims trend and consumer spending data
Regulatory developments in AI (Anthropic's call for pause) and private credit
EU's call for Spain to cut gas use after blackout
India's bond tax scrapping and foreign investment flows
watch_list: Fed funds futures and rate hike probability
Nasdaq 100 vs Dow Jones ratio (rotation indicator)
Oil prices and Hormuz shipping data
SpaceX IPO demand and aftermarket performance
AI-related credit deal volume (Morgan Stanley metric)
Geopolitical headlines on US-Iran and Middle East
